content: Diagnosis requires excisional biopsy with narrow margins (1-3mm) for suspicious lesions. ABCDE criteria: Asymmetry, Border irregularity, Color variation, Diameter >6mm, Evolution (change). Pathology report must include: Breslow thickness (mm), ulceration (present/absent), mitotic rate (/mm²), margins, microsatellitosis, lymphovascular invasion. Staging: AJCC 8th edition - T category based on thickness and ulceration; N category based on number of involved nodes, micromacrometastasis, matted nodes; M category based on site of metastasis (skin/subcutaneous M1a, lung M1b, other visceral M1c, CNS M1d) and LDH level. Sentinel lymph node biopsy: recommended for T2-T4 tumors, considered for T1b (0.8-1.0mm or <0.8mm with ulceration); identifies occult nodal disease, provides prognostic information. Imaging: CT/PET-CT for stage III-IV or symptomatic patients; brain MRI for stage IV or neurologic symptoms. LDH elevation indicates poor prognosis in metastatic disease.
